name: Pita Bread - Part 2
recipeInstructions:
1. The dough will smell slightly fermented after a few days, but this simply improves the taste of the bread.
2. Bring the dough to room temperature before baking.
3. This amount of dough will make approximately 16 pitas if rolled out into circles about 8 to 9 inches in diameter and less than 1/4 inch thick. You can also make smaller breads; size and shape depend on you.
4. Place unglazed quarry tiles or a large baking stone (or two baking sheets) on a rack in the bottom third of your oven, leaving a 1-inch gap all around to allow air circulation.
5. Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C).
6. Divide the dough in half; set one side aside, covered, while you work with the other half.
7. Divide the remaining dough into eight equal pieces and flatten each piece with lightly floured hands.
8. Roll out each piece to a circle about 8 to 9 inches in diameter. You may wish to roll out all eight before starting to bake.
9. Cover rolled-out breads, but do not stack them.
10. Bake the pitas in batches of 2 or more directly on quarry tiles or baking sheets.
11. Bake each pita for 3 to 4 minutes, until it has gone into a full "balloon" puffing or until it starts turning lightly golden, whichever happens first.
12. If seams or dry bits of dough cause the bread not to fully balloon, do not worry—it will still taste great.
13. When baked, remove from oven and place on a rack for about five minutes to let cool slightly; then wrap in a large kitchen towel to keep them soft.
14. Repeat steps 6-13 with the reserved half of dough or store refrigerated for later use. You can also divide the dough into smaller pieces for smaller breads.
Yield: Approximately 16 pitas, each about 8 inches in diameter, or more smaller breads.
